fields.comment: Azul has a really elegant setup and the tile drafting can feel satisfying when you pull off a good pattern, but it can also swing between feeling very tactical and a bit punishing if things don’t go your way early on. The downtime between turns in larger groups can drag slightly, and sometimes the experience feels more reactive than strategic. Still, it’s a beautifully produced game that works well for casual play, even if it doesn’t quite stay exciting over repeated sessions.
